requirement: Data subjects have the right to meaningful information about the logic of automated decisions affecting them.
implementation: SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values are computed for every prediction made by the XGBoost model. SHAP provides mathematically guaranteed, locally accurate explanations grounded in cooperative game theory (Lundberg & Lee, 2017).
explainability_method: TreeExplainer — exact, model-native SHAP for tree-based models. No approximation required.

global_explanation.description: Ranked feature importance by mean absolute SHAP value across all test predictions.

local_explanation.description: Individual waterfall and force plots generated for each prediction on request. Each plot shows the exact contribution of every feature to that specific decision.

right_to_contest: The system logs prediction probabilities and feature contributions. Any applicant may request an explanation of a credit decision, satisfying Article 22(3).
reference: Lundberg, S. M., & Lee, S.-I. (2017). A unified approach to interpreting model predictions. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30.
